subject: Human Resources
title: Role Mismatch and Internal Mobility
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Role Mismatch and Internal Mobility.

hook.text: A brilliant engineer is promoted to manage a team. She hates it. Her code was flawless—her management isn't. She's not failing.

buildup.text: Role mismatch happens when strengths don't align with the current position—not a performance issue but a placement one.

discovery.text: Internal mobility—moving people to roles that fit their strengths—saves talent that termination would waste.

twist.text: Many companies treat lateral moves as failures. The best companies treat them as strategic—matching strengths to needs.

climax.text: Honest conversations about fit—'You're talented, but this role isn't right'—preserve both the person and the relationship.

punchline.text: Wrong role doesn't mean wrong person. Sometimes the fix is a move, not an exit.

quiz.question: What is role mismatch?
options: When someone's strengths don't align with their current position
When someone is unqualified for any role
